column.name: title
column.value: Partner — Regional Strategy & Investment Planning (North America)

column.name: meta_title
column.value: Partner · North America Strategy & Investment Planning · IMan Investment

column.name: meta_description
column.value: Lead North America regional strategy, policy-aware opportunity mapping and investment planning for IMan Investment.

insert.column:
IMan Investment is appointing a Partner for Regional Strategy & Investment Planning covering North America. You will interpret federal, state and municipal policy environments, identify development opportunities, and translate them into actionable investment theses, partnership pathways and capital allocation priorities.
This Partner-level mandate combines public-policy fluency with investment judgement — shaping how IMan engages founders, corporates and institutions across the United States and Canada.

• Own IMan’s North America regional strategy and multi-year investment planning agenda • Monitor government policy, industrial incentives, trade frameworks and regulatory shifts that create investable opportunities • Map sector theses (AI, healthcare, climate, fintech, advanced manufacturing) against regional policy catalysts • Originate equity investment and strategic partnership opportunities with early-stage and growth companies • Build relationships with economic development agencies, ecosystem builders and institutional stakeholders • Produce regional opportunity briefs and recommendations for the investment committee and firm leadership • Coordinate with Hong Kong and New York teams on cross-border capital, market entry and co-investment paths • Represent IMan at selected policy, industry and capital forums across North America

• 10+ years in venture capital, private equity, corporate strategy, public policy / economic development, or related Partner-track roles • Demonstrated ability to convert policy and market context into concrete investment or partnership recommendations • Deep North America network across founders, corporates, advisors and/or government-adjacent institutions • Strong written strategy materials and board-level communication skills • Comfort operating as a senior remote / hybrid leader with accountability for regional outcomes • Bachelor’s degree required; MBA, MPP or equivalent advanced degree preferred • Prior US or Canada market leadership experience strongly preferred

• Partner-level compensation with performance and carried / bonus participation as applicable • Hybrid or remote flexibility across North America • Mandate ownership for a core global region • Direct partnership with firm leadership on capital allocation and platform strategy • Travel budget for regional coverage and cross-border collaboration • Opportunity to shape IMan’s long-term North America presence

column.name: title
column.value: Partner — Regional Strategy & Investment Planning (Middle East)

column.name: meta_title
column.value: Partner · Middle East Strategy & Investment Planning · IMan Investment

column.name: meta_description
column.value: Lead Middle East regional strategy under evolving government and diversification agendas for IMan Investment.

insert.column:
IMan Investment is appointing a Partner for Regional Strategy & Investment Planning covering the Middle East. You will navigate national diversification agendas, sovereign and quasi-sovereign ecosystems, and regulatory frameworks to identify high-conviction development opportunities across technology, energy transition, logistics, fintech and related themes.
The role requires a Partner who can operate with cultural fluency, policy awareness and investment discipline across GCC markets and broader Middle East corridors.

• Define and lead IMan’s Middle East regional strategy and investment planning roadmap • Analyse government visions, incentive programmes, free-zone frameworks and sector priorities for opportunity signals • Identify equity investment, co-investment and strategic partnership pathways with regional champions and global entrants • Engage sovereign-linked entities, family offices, accelerators and corporate ventures where relevant • Prepare regional theses, risk assessments and investment recommendations for firm leadership • Support founders seeking Middle East market entry or capital relationships through IMan’s network • Coordinate with Asia Pacific and North America teams on cross-border structuring and partnership design • Represent IMan at key regional forums while maintaining rigorous opportunity filtering

• 10+ years across investing, corporate development, government relations / economic strategy, or regional Partner roles • Proven experience interpreting Middle East policy and institutional environments into commercial outcomes • Established relationships across one or more GCC markets preferred • Excellent judgement on geopolitical, regulatory and counterparty risk • Strong English communication; Arabic language ability is a significant advantage • Bachelor’s degree required; advanced degree preferred • Willingness to travel within the region and collaborate across global time zones

• Partner-level compensation package with performance alignment • Hybrid / remote flexibility with regional travel expectations • Ownership of IMan’s Middle East strategy platform • Access to cross-border deal flow and Asia–Gulf connectivity • Professional development and relationship-building support • Opportunity to build a durable regional franchise for the firm

column.name: title
column.value: Partner — Regional Strategy & Investment Planning (Southeast Asia)

column.name: meta_title
column.value: Partner · Southeast Asia Strategy & Investment Planning · IMan Investment

column.name: meta_description
column.value: Lead Southeast Asia regional strategy and policy-informed investment planning for IMan Investment.

insert.column:
IMan Investment is appointing a Partner for Regional Strategy & Investment Planning covering Southeast Asia. You will work across ASEAN policy environments, digital economy agendas and industrial development programmes to uncover investment and strategic partnership opportunities for early-stage and growth companies.
Based preferably in Singapore or another major ASEAN hub — with hybrid / remote flexibility — you will connect regional government and market dynamics to IMan’s Asia Pacific investment platform.

• Own Southeast Asia regional strategy and investment planning for IMan Investment • Track ASEAN policy, digitalisation, infrastructure, fintech regulation and industrial incentive programmes • Translate government and market catalysts into sector theses and opportunity pipelines • Source equity investment and strategic cooperation opportunities across Singapore, Indonesia, Vietnam, Thailand, Malaysia and related markets • Cultivate relationships with founders, corporates, development institutions and ecosystem partners • Deliver clear regional briefs and investment recommendations to Partners and the investment committee • Collaborate closely with Hong Kong leadership on capital allocation and cross-border support for portfolio companies • Represent IMan in regional strategy dialogues while maintaining high diligence standards

• 10+ years in venture capital, growth investing, corporate strategy, public policy / market development, or Partner-level roles in Southeast Asia • Deep understanding of ASEAN market heterogeneity and policy-driven opportunity formation • Strong founder and institutional network in at least two major Southeast Asian markets • Excellent written and verbal English; Bahasa Indonesia, Vietnamese, Thai or Mandarin a plus • Ability to lead remotely / hybrid with frequent regional travel • Bachelor’s degree required; MBA or public policy background preferred • Experience bridging Southeast Asia with Greater China or global capital is highly valued

• Partner-level compensation with performance participation • Hybrid / remote flexibility across Southeast Asia hubs • Clear ownership of a priority growth region for the firm • Close collaboration with Hong Kong investment leadership • Travel and ecosystem engagement support • Opportunity to institutionalise IMan’s long-term ASEAN presence

column.name: meta_title
column.value: Partner · Europe Strategy & Investment Planning · IMan Investment

column.name: meta_description
column.value: Lead Europe regional strategy under EU and national policy frameworks for IMan Investment.

insert.column:
IMan Investment is appointing a Partner for Regional Strategy & Investment Planning covering Europe. You will analyse EU and national policy environments — including industrial strategy, climate regulation, digital markets and innovation funding — to identify development opportunities and shape IMan’s European investment and partnership agenda.
This Partner role suits a senior leader who can connect policy complexity with practical capital deployment and cross-border collaboration between Europe and Asia.

• Lead IMan’s Europe regional strategy and multi-year investment planning • Interpret EU and member-state policy, funding instruments and regulatory regimes for opportunity creation • Develop theses across deep tech, climate, healthcare, enterprise software and related European strengths • Originate equity investment and strategic partnership opportunities with European founders and scale-ups • Engage innovation agencies, corporates, funds and institutional stakeholders across priority markets • Prepare regional strategy papers and investment recommendations for firm leadership • Coordinate with Hong Kong and New York teams on Asia–Europe and US–Europe corridor opportunities • Represent IMan selectively across European capital and policy forums

• 10+ years in venture capital, growth equity, corporate strategy, EU / public affairs adjacent roles, or Partner-level investing • Ability to convert European policy and market structure into investable theses • Strong network across at least two major European markets (e.g. UK, France, Germany, Nordics, Netherlands) • Excellent English; additional European languages strongly preferred • Comfortable with hybrid / remote leadership and cross-border travel • Bachelor’s degree required; advanced degree preferred • Experience with Asia–Europe capital or market linkages is a plus

• Partner-level compensation with performance alignment • Hybrid / remote flexibility across European locations • Ownership of Europe as a core regional platform • Cross-border collaboration with Asia Pacific and North America teams • Travel and professional network support • Opportunity to define IMan’s enduring European strategy
